items.title: Rising Demand for Hedges Hints at Volatility Ahead: Taking Stock
items.description: The momentum trade carried along by the equity rally is faltering, while optimistic earnings expectations are under intense scrutiny, two of the signs pointing to a volatile summer.

items.title: Segro Rejects Fresh £13.5 Billion Cash and Share Prologis Offer
items.description: Segro Plc has rejected the latest proposal from Prologis Inc. that offered about £13.5 billion for the UK warehouse landlord.

items.title: China Is Exporting 20% Fewer Magnets to US Despite Trade Truce
items.description: China’s shipments of rare-earth magnets to the US remain well below pre-trade war levels despite last year’s truce, underscoring concerns in the Trump administration about Beijing’s adherence to that

items.title: Octopus-Backed MOPO Partners Nigeria in $75 Million Battery Plan
items.description: MOPO, an Africa-focused battery rental company backed by Octopus Energy Group Ltd., said it’s signed an agreement with Nigeria to facilitate a $75 million expansion of its activities in Africa’s most

items.title: FTSE 100 Live: Oil Back to $90 to Keep Inflation Pressures Rumbling
China Solar Stocks Rise After Beijing Issues Consumption Tax
items.description: Shares of most Chinese solar and major battery makers rose on Monday after Beijing unveiled a consumption tax on solar cells and lithium batteries, raising hopes it will hasten industry consolidation,

items.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Mixed as Investors Weigh Iran Tensions
items.description: Emerging-market stocks and currencies were mixed as investors weighed rising tensions between the US and Iran, as well as a technology-led selloff that rattled global markets last week.

items.title: Palm Climbs to Near One-Month High as Iran Conflict Boosts Crude
items.description: Palm oil rose to the highest in nearly a month, tracking renewed strength in crude and other vegetable oil prices as the US and Iran exchanged escalating military strikes.

items.title: RBI Intervenes to Support Rupee as Currency Nears Record Low
items.description: India’s central bank stepped into the foreign exchange markets to support the rupee after the currency weakened toward a record low as a renewed surge in crude prices weighed on sentiment.

items.title: Key China Copper Gauge Rallies to $100 After Tax Crackdown
items.description: A closely-watched gauge of China’s copper market has risen to the highest in more than a year, as a tax shake-up in the metal’s biggest market spurs a shortage of scrap and boosts demand for imports.

items.title: NZD: Hedge Funds Amass Biggest New Zealand Dollar Net Short Since 2006
items.description: Leveraged funds have taken their short positions on the New Zealand dollar to a record high, betting that a recent rebound in global oil prices may exacerbate domestic economic pressures.

items.title: Innolight Gauges Interest for $8 Billion Hong Kong Listing
items.description: China’s Zhongji Innolight Co. is starting to gauge investor demand for its Hong Kong listing, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News, bringing it a step closer to what may be the city’s

items.title: Tungsten Miner’s Stock Jumps as Billionaire Forrest Buys Stake
items.description: EQ Resources Ltd. shares surged after mining billionaire Andrew Forrest agreed to acquire a 16.8% stake in the Sydney-listed tungsten producer from Oaktree Capital Management LP.

items.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Assess Rates Path After US-Iran Clashes
items.description: Gold steadied after the US and Iran ramped up attacks over the weekend, as traders assess the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to contain inflation.

items.title: Oil Rises as US-Iran Attacks Intensify, Bonds Fall: Markets Wrap
items.description: Oil climbed to the highest level in more than a month and bonds fell as US and Iranian attacks escalated, renewing inflation concerns. Stocks steadied after a technology-led selloff rattled markets la

items.description: Brent crude jumped after the US and Iran escalated hostilities, including the targeting of vessels attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz and an attack on a key oil facility in Kuwait over the wee

items.title: Safest Carry in Emerging Markets Is in Latin American Currencies
items.description: Emerging-market currency volatility has dropped to the lowest since the start of year and that’s re-energizing carry trades, particularly those involving Latin America that are seen to offer the juici

items.title: US Strikes Iran in Escalating Campaign After Troops Killed
items.description: The US and Iran engaged in a quickening series of tit-for-tat attacks, with the American military conducting a wave of strikes on the Islamic Republic following the deaths of service personnel.

items.title: South Korea Details Plan to Make Won Freely Tradable Abroad
items.description: South Korea laid out a detailed plan to make the won freely tradable among foreigners, its boldest step yet to liberalize the foreign-exchange market.

items.title: GLP-1 Opportunity to Lift Domestic Business at Indian Pharma Firms
